CONTENT.Club career: On 20 June 2019, Brassier signed a professional contract with Rennes and immediately went on loan with Valenciennes.He made his professional debut with Valenciennes in a 1–1 Ligue 2 tie with Nancy on 2 August 2019.In 2020, Brassier signed for Brest on loan with an option to buy if the club stayed up in Ligue 1. On 13 March 2021, he scored his first Ligue 1 goal in a 3–1 defeat against Marseille. His loan deal was made permanent after Brest secured safety on the final matchday of the season.In the 2023–24 season, he contributed to his club's impressive campaign, as they secured a third-place finish in the league and first ever qualification to the UEFA Champions League.On 3 July 2024, Brassier signed with Olympique Marseille on loan with an option to buy.
CONTENT.External links: Lilian Brassier at SoccerwayLilian Brassier – French league stats at LFP – also available in French (archived)Lilian Brassier – French league stats at Ligue 1 – also available in FrenchFFF Profile
CONTENT.International career: Born in France, Brassier is of Togolese descent. He is a youth international for France.

SUMMARY: Lilian Brassier (born 2 November 1999) is a French professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for Ligue 1 club Marseille, on loan from Brest.
